Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 每个前端开发人员都需要的免费图像工具列表 | List of Free Image Tools Every Frontend Developer Needs
item.description:
这是每个前端开发人员可能会在 Google 上搜索一天的免费工具列表。 这些网站可让您调整大小、重新格式化、编辑、生成或下载图像和视频以在您的网站或博客上使用。

它是一个非常强大的图像编辑工具，也可以用来将图像转换为不同的格式，同时改变图像的质量。 通过图像工具栏菜单调整灯光和颜色非常容易。 只需谷歌如何在 Photoshop 中做你想做的事，Photopea 通常会以类似的方式工作。 只有您的幻想限制了您可以使用 Photopea 做什么。

您可以通过查找互补色、相似色或阴影色轻松创建自己的主题，甚至可以从图像中提取主题。 您可以有效地使用 HSB、LAB 和 RGB 颜色并检查颜色之间的对比度。 这是我多年来一直使用的工具。


如前所述，Adobe Color Wheel 允许您从图像中提取调色板主题。 它不是唯一允许您这样做的工具。 如果您对结果不满意，您可以使用Picsart 的工具。


不想自己创建主题调色板？ 你可以生成一个！

听起来没什么用，但有时你实际上需要 Base64 格式的纯色图片，那么你可以使用PNG Pixel 。 有时可以将微小的 Base64 图像作为 CSS 直接嵌入，网站会向您展示如何操作，半透明彩色背景图像可以在延迟加载图像时将其用作模糊图像占位符时提供良好的用户体验。 您可以在我网站的 meme page上查看它的外观。

如果您发现使用 Base64 图像很有用， Base64 Guru允许您将图像、音频和其他文件类型转换为 Base64 格式。 它可能不是日常使用的工具，但是当您需要它时，很高兴知道它。

您想要一个简单的纯 CSS 背景为您的网站吗？ 选择一种图案并选择一种颜色和不透明度，您就完成了。 我为您提供了可以复制粘贴的生成 CSS 代码。 另一个类似的站点是BG Jar 。

或者为什么不使用Waterpipe.js生成酷炫的烟熏背景图像？


吸烟可能有害，但烟熏背景是完全安全的！

足够的工具来生成背景。 也许你甚至不想要背景。 如果是这样，也有工具！ 当然，老金 Adob​​e Express 也可以删除背景。 请务必查看 Adob​​e Express 的其他免费工具，他们有很多。


奶奶想要她的背景回来

免版税图片可以在不同的网站上找到。 其中一些需要花钱，其中一些需要署名说明，另一些则完全免费下载，请务必阅读该特定图像的许可证。 这里有一些选择。

  • 关注
  • 费皮克
  • 不飞溅
  • 像素
  • 股票快照

还有一些网站提供您可以自己定制的插图。

您需要视频而不是图像吗？ 当然，这也存在！


搜索并滚动查找您从未知道自己需要的图像

每个网页都需要一个图标。 为什么不直接生成呢？ 无需逐个像素地绘制它，您可以从图像中生成一个！ 该工具支持为 Web 和移动设备上的不同平台创建图标。 不幸的是，这个工具使用起来有点慢，所以如果你想要一个快速简单的选项，你可以使用favicon.io ，它允许你从图像、表情符号和文本中生成一个 favicon。

您需要网站的个人资料图片吗？ 您可以使用其中一种工具生成一个。

如果您想生成头像而不是上传自己的照片，您可以使用其中一种工具。

  • 头像制作者
  • 随机用户


我们应该怎么称呼她？ 在评论中建议一个名字！

一些工具可以用于很多事情。 这些是您绝对应该检查并保存以备后用的工具。

  • 网页代码工具

在哪里了解更多信息

如果你喜欢这篇文章，你可能有兴趣阅读类似的文章。 您可以在 DEV 上执行此操作，也可以查看我的网站。 我在Instagram 上也很活跃，在那里我发布了更多的程序员模因。 觉得有趣的话记得关注我哦！



原文:

This is a list of free tools every frontend developer probably will google for one day. These websites lets you resize, reformat, edit, generating or downloading images and videos to use on your website or blog.

1. Photopea - Online Photoshop Web Client

Photopea is like Photoshop, but online. It's a very powerful tool for editing images and can also be used to convert images into different formats while changing the quality of the images. Adjusting lights and colors is very easy to do via the Image toolbar menu. Just google how to do what you want in Photoshop and Photopea will often work in a similar way. Only your fantasy restricts what you can do with Photopea.

2. Adobe Color Wheel - Working with Colors

Adobe Color Wheel has great tools for working with colors. You can easily create your own theme by finding complementary, analogous or shade colors, or even extract a theme from an image. You can work effectively with HSB, LAB and RGB colors and check contrasts between colors. It's a tool I have used a lot through out the years.


Adobe Color Wheel is a great tool to use when working with theme colors

3. Generating a Theme From an Image

As mentioned, Adobe Color Wheel lets you to extract a color palette theme from an image. It's not the only tool that allows you to do that. If you are not satisfied with the result you can use Picsart's tool for that.


Don't wanna create a theme palette on your own? You can generate one!

4. Generating Solid Color Background Images in Base64

Sounds pretty useless, but sometimes you actually need solid color pictures in Base64 format, then you can use PNG Pixel. Tiny Base64 images can sometime be embedded directly as CSS which the site shows you how to do, and a semi-transparent colored background image can give a nice user experience when using it as a blurred image placeholder while lazy loading an image. You can check out what it looks like on my website's meme page.

5. Turning Images and Files to Base64

If you find it useful to work with Base64 images, Base64 Guru allows you to convert images, audio and other file types into Base64 format. It's maybe not a tool to use daily, but when you need it it's nice to know about it.

6. Generating CSS Background Images

Do you want a simple CSS-only background for your website? Hero Patterns allows you to generate SVG files you can use directly as CSS backgrounds. Choose a pattern and select a color and opacity and you are done. I provides you with generated CSS code ready to be copy pasted. Another similar site is BG Jar.

Or why not generate a cool smokey background image with Waterpipe.js?


Smoking can be harmful, but smokey backgrounds are totally safe!

7. Removing Background From an Image

Enough of tools for generating backgrounds. Maybe you don't even want a background. If so, there are tools for that as well! Picsart has a background remover and remove.bg is dedicated for that. And of course, old gold' Adobe Express can remove backgrounds as well. Make sure to checkout Adobe Express' other free tools, they have many of them.


Grandma wants her background back

8. Converting Images Into Different Formats

Convertio allows you to convert images, videos, audio and other kind of documents into different formats. Adobe Express can also convert images and videos, but they don't support as many formats as Convertio.


Convertio offers to convert between plenty of formats

9. Royalty Free Images, Graphics and Videos

Royalty free images can be found at different sites. Some of them costs money, some of them requires an attribution note, others are completely free to download, make sure to read the licence for that specific image. Here are a few alternatives.

  • Pixabay
  • Feepik
  • Unsplash
  • Picjumbo
  • Pexels
  • StockSnap

There are also sites that offer illustrations that you can customize on your own.

Do you need a video instead of an image? Sure, that exists too!


Search and scroll to find images you never knew you needed

10. Generate a Favicon

Every web page needs a favicon. Why not generate one directly? There's no need to draw it pixel by pixel, you can generate one from an image! That tool supports creating icons for different platforms on both web and mobile. The tool is unfortunately a bit slow to use, so if you want a quick and easy option you can use favicon.io which allows you to generate a favicon from images, emojis and texts.

11. Create a Profile Picture From an Image

Do you want need a profile picture for you site? You can generate one using one of these tools.

If you would want to generate an avatar rather than uploading your own photo, you can use one of these tools.

  • Avatar Maker
  • Random Users


What should we call her? Suggest a name in the comments!

12. Multi Purpose Tools

Some tools can be used for a lot of things. These ones are tools you definitely should check out and save for later.

  • Adobe Express
  • Picsart
  • Web Code Tools

Where To Learn More

If you liked this article, you are maybe interested to read similar articles. You can do that here on DEV or by checking out my website. I'm also active on Instagram where I post more programmer memes. Make sure to follow me if you find it interesting!
item.author: perssondennis

item.title: 单元测试前端代码（可能）没用 | Unit testing frontend code is (probably) useless
item.description:
托马斯·汉森

发表于我喜欢单元测试。 使用自动化静态分析代码对我们来说很重要。 然而，单元测试前端代码坦率地说（可能）很荒谬。

单元测试适用于小逻辑单元，以自动确保您的代码在重构时（仍然）有效，和/或向其添加功能。 如果您将需要单元测试的逻辑放入前端代码中，您可能做错了什么。

在我的职业生涯中，90% 的项目都是企业管理工具，和/或面向客户的网站、集线器和注册表单等。如果你将业务逻辑放入这些前端项目，你可能会冒一些聪明的家伙改变的风险他的 JSON 有效负载，绕过您的业务逻辑，访问不应访问的内容。 因此，您应该将业务逻辑、流程和验证器等放入后端代码中。 在您的前端复制一些较小的部分，例如让验证器防止传输虚假有效负载以节省带宽是可以的 - 但是在您的前端添加业务逻辑到您的前端需要单元测试以验证其逻辑仍然有效的点是要么是冗余的代码重复，以至于你已经有效地“两次”地创建了你的应用程序，或者如果你只在前端这样做，坦率地说是疯狂的！

有一些例外，例如需要大量复杂前端代码的前端繁重的应用程序。 一个例子是来自现实世界的Canva ，它的 95% 的“价值主张”都在前端，允许人们完全从头开始创建图像、演示文稿，几乎就像 Photo Shop 的简化版一样。 顺便说一句，如果你还没有尝试过的话，Canva 是一个很棒的工具。 但是，大多数企业类型的应用程序不应在其前端包含任何数量的业务逻辑或其他类型的逻辑，需要进行单元测试以确保有效性。 而且，如果您在此类项目中进行了单元测试，对其进行修改或维护，那么单元测试只会变得更加困难。 而且无论您的项目中有多少单元测试，它们都永远不会取代集成测试和/或人工完成的 QA 测试。

每次我创建一个新的 Angular 组件时，我做的第一件事就是删除“规范”文件——因为如果我首先需要它，可能有人应该扇我一巴掌……；）

洛伦佐·扎兰托内洛 - 一旦暂停，polterguy 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，polterguy 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，polterguy 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 polterguy 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 polterguy 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 polterguy 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Thomas Hansen

Posted on Jul 31

I love unit tests. Magic and its satellite projects has 1,000+ unit tests, and some of the satellite projects even has 100% unit test coverage. Statically analysing code using automation is (duh!) kind of important to us. However, unit testing frontend code is quite frankly (probably) ridiculous.

Unit tests are for small units of logic, to automatically ensure that your code is (still) valid as you refactor it, and/or add features to it. If you put logic that requires unit testing into your frontend code, you are probably doing something wrong.

90% of all projects I have worked with in my professional life were enterprise administration tools, and/or customer facing websites, hubs, and registration forms, etc. If you put business logic into these frontend projects, you risk that some smart dude changes his JSON payloads, circumventing your business logic, accessing things that shouldn't be possible to access. You should therefor put your business logic, flows, and validators etc in your backend code. Duplicating some smaller parts of this in your frontend, such as having validators preventing a bogus payload being transmitted to conserve bandwidth is OK - But adding business logic in your frontend to the point where your frontend requires unit testing to verify its logic is still working is either redundant duplication of code to the point where you've effectively created your app "twice", or if you only do it in the frontend quite frankly madness!

There are some exceptions to this such as frontend heavy applications that requires extreme amounts of complex frontend code. An example would be Canva from the real world, that has 95% of its "value proposition" in the frontend, allowing people to create images, presentations, entirely from scratch, almost like a dumbed down version of Photo Shop. BTW, Canva is an awesome tool if you haven't tried it yet. However, most enterprise types of apps should not have any amount of business logic, and or other types of logic in their frontends, requiring unit testing to ensure validity. And if you've got unit tests in such projects, modifying them, and or maintaining them, only becomes harder as a result of your unit tests. And regardless of how many unit tests you have in your project, they will never replace integration testing, and/or QA testing done by human beings.

The first thing I do every time I create a new Angular component, is to delete the "spec" file - Because if I needed it in the first place, somebody should probably slap me in my face ... ;)

Lorenzo Zarantonello - Jul 7

Once unsuspended, polterguy will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by polterguy will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If polterguy is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 干净的代码在你的创业成功中的重要性 | The Importance of Clean Code in Your Startup's Success
item.description:
清洁代码最近成为了一个流行词，但无论是作为开发人员还是作为初创公司的执行官或 CTO，它都很重要。 投资者希望看到您的初创公司高效运行，并且您对代码库有很好的了解。 如果你的代码是干净的，他们就会更容易理解你的创业公司是做什么的以及它是如何工作的。

这可以让他们有信心投资您的公司。 作为一名开发人员，根据我自己的经验，编写干净的代码，尤其是在每周尝试新想法的初创公司中，当你不可避免地不得不回去减少技术债务或只是修复一些问题时，保持代码的干净将对你有所帮助.
客户想知道他们的数据是安全的，并且您的产品是可靠的。 如果他们看到您的代码组织良好且易于阅读，他们将更有可能信任您的产品。 此外，如果您有干净的代码，您将更容易进行更改和添加功能。

合作伙伴希望与组织有序且对其技术有深入了解的初创公司合作。 如果您的初创公司有干净的代码，那么潜在合作伙伴将更容易评估您的技术能力并决定与您合作是否对他们的业务有意义。
干净的代码对于任何创业公司的成功都是至关重要的。 通过从一开始就投资干净的代码，从长远来看，您将节省时间和金钱，同时让您的初创公司对潜在投资者、客户和合作伙伴更具吸引力。

如何编写干净的代码

编写干净代码的第一步是保持简单。 这意味着使用清晰简洁的变量和函数名称。 这也意味着避免不必要的复杂性。 目标是使您的代码尽可能易于阅读和理解。 第二个提示是避免重复自己（DRY）。 这意味着不要不必要地复制代码。 重复的代码更难维护并且可能导致错误。 如果您需要在多个地方使用同一段代码，请为其创建一个函数或类。 这将使您的代码更干燥，更易于维护。

第三个技巧是使用注释来解释你的代码。 注释对于使您的代码具有可读性和可理解性很重要。 但是，应该谨慎使用它们，因为过多的注释会使您的代码难以阅读。 使用评论来解释你为什么做某事，而不是你在做什么。

干净代码对创业成功的重要性

初创公司的目标应该是保持低圈复杂度，因为从长远来看，这将为他们节省时间和金钱。 有很多方法可以做到这一点，例如使用注释和清除变量名。 此外，初创公司应使用编码约定和样式指南，以确保其代码一致且易于阅读。

永远不要假设有人会理解一个聪明的解决方案，甚至一个月后你自己也不会，总是用之前提到的几句话来记录你的片段，并充分利用变量名，即使它们有点长（我有太多变量在我自己被遗忘的代码中，例如 temp1、temp2、temp_df、temp_nuke_code_base 等等！）

干净的代码对创业公司成功的重要性不可低估。 不干净的代码不仅会导致代价高昂的错误和错误，还会使您的代码更难阅读和理解。 这可能会对您的创业公司的底线产生重大影响，因为如果代码不干净，开发人员将需要花费更多时间来处理您的代码。

有许多不同的方法来衡量代码质量，但一个常见的指标是“圈复杂度”。 这衡量了一段代码可以采用的不同路径的数量。 圈复杂度越高，代码越难理解和维护。 因此，初创公司应该致力于保持低圈复杂度，以节省时间和金钱。

有多种方法可以实现这一目标。 例如，初创公司可以使用注释和清晰的变量名来使他们的代码更具可读性。 此外，他们应该使用编码约定和样式指南，以确保他们的代码一致且易于阅读。 通过遵循这些最佳实践，初创公司可以确保他们的代码干净且易于维护——无论是现在还是将来。

综上所述：

  1. 代码应该组织良好且易于阅读。
  2. 代码应该是模块化的，以便于更改或添加新功能。
  3. 代码应该经过很好的测试，这样错误就不太可能发生。
  4. 代码应该有很好的文档记录，以便其他人可以轻松理解它。
  5. 代码应该是一致的，以便于维护。
  6. 代码应该没有任何错误或警告（在合理范围内）。
  7. 代码应该是高性能的，这样它才能快速有效地运行。
  8. 代码应该是安全的，这样它才能抵抗攻击或利用。

如果你的创业公司的代码不够完美，那么你注定要失败。 确保您的代码是干净的，否则您的启动将付诸东流。

为我们的Github 存储库加注星标并加入我们的Discord 频道中的讨论！
现在在BLST免费测试您的 API！

波格丹·亚历山德鲁·米利塔鲁 - 一旦暂停，chainguns 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，chainguns 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，chainguns 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果chainguns 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记链枪：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记链枪将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Clean Code has become a bit of a buzzword recently, but it is important, both as a developer and as an executive or CTO in a startup. Investors want to see that your startup is run efficiently and that you have a good understanding of your code base. If your code is clean, it will be easier for them to understand what your startup does and how it works.

This can give them the confidence they need to invest in your company. As a developer, and from my own experience, writing clean code, especially in a startup where new ideas are tried every week, keeping your code clean will help you when you inevetiablly have to go back and reduce your tech debt or just fix some things.
Customers want to know that their data is safe and that your product is reliable. If they see that your code is wellorganized and easy to read, they’ll be more likely to trust your product. Additionally, if you have clean code, it’ll be easier for you to make changes and add features down the line.

Partners want to work with startups that are organized and have a good understanding of their technology. If your startup has clean code, it will be easier for potential partners to assess your technical capabilities and decide if working with you makes sense for their business.
clean code is crucial for the success of any startup. By investing in clean code from the start, you’ll save yourself time and money in the long run while making your startup more attractive to potential investors, customers, and partners.

How to write clean code

The first step to writing clean code is to keep it simple. This means using clear and concise variable and function names. It also means avoiding unnecessary complexity. The goal is to make your code as easy to read and understand as possible. The second tip is to avoid repeating yourself (DRY). This means not duplicating code unnecessarily. Duplicated code is more difficult to maintain and can lead to errors. If you need to use the same piece of code in multiple places, create a function or class for it. This will make your code more DRY and easier to maintain.

The third tip is to use comments to explain your code. Comments are important for making your code readable and understandable. They should be used sparingly, however, as too many comments can make your code hard to read. Use comments to explain why you’re doing something, not what you’re doing.

The importance of clean code in your startup's success

Startups should aim to keep their cyclomatic complexity low, as this will save them time and money in the long run. There are a number of ways to do this, such as using comments and clear variable names. In addition, startups should use coding conventions and style guides to ensure that their code is consistent and easy to read.

Never assume someone will understand a clever solution, not even yourself in a month from now, always document your snippets with a few words like mention before, and make good use of variable names even if they are a bit long ( I have too many variables in my own code that are forgotten like temp1, temp2, temp_df, temp_nuke_code_base and more!)

The importance of clean code in your startup's success cannot be understated. Not only can unclean code lead to costly errors and bugs, but it can also make your code more difficult to read and understand. This can have a major impact on your startup's bottom line, as developers will need to spend more time working on your code if it is not clean.

There are a number of different ways to measure code quality, but one common metric is the "cyclomatic complexity." This measures the number of different paths that a piece of code can take. The higher the cyclomatic complexity, the more difficult it is to understand and maintain the code. As a result, startups should aim to keep their cyclomatic complexity low in order to save time and money.

There are a number of ways to achieve this goal. For example, startups can use comments and clear variable names to make their code more readable. In addition, they should use coding conventions and style guides to ensure that their code is consistent and easy to read. By following these best practices, startups can ensure that their code is clean and easy to maintain – both now and in the future.

In conclusion:

  1. Code should be well organized and easy to read.
  2. Code should be modular so that it is easy to change or add new features.
  3. Code should be well tested so that bugs are less likely to occur.
  4. Code should be well documented so that others can understand it easily.
  5. Code should be consistent so that it is easy to maintain.
  6. Code should be free of any errors or warnings (within reason).
  7. Code should be performant so that it runs quickly and efficiently.
  8. Code should be secure so that it is resistant to attack or exploitation.

If your startup's code is anything less than perfect, you're doomed to fail. Make sure your code is clean, otherwise your startup is going down the drain.

Star our Github repo and join the discussion in our Discord channel!
Test your API for free now at BLST!

Bogdan Alexandru Militaru - Jul 13

item.title: 使您的 Web 应用程序国际化的最有效方式 | The most efficient way to internationalize your web app
item.description:
开发人员在本地化方面遇到了很多麻烦。 有时很难创建和管理本地化字符串、处理外部库等等。 拥有一个管理一切并使翻译工作更轻松的一站式商店怎么样？

让我们开始吧

我经常写关于令人惊叹的开源项目。 今天我想向你介绍Tolgee ，另一个很棒的开源工具。 包括本地化项目所需的一切。 让我们看看如何在本文中充分利用 Tolgee。

在 Tolgee 中创建项目

步骤1：

  • 打开 Tolgee应用程序。 您可以使用您的电子邮件和密码加入，也可以立即通过 Github 验证您的帐户。

第2步：

  • 然后，您将能够看到项目仪表板； 单击位于该仪表板右上角的 + 添加。

第 3 步：

  • 命名您的项目，选择所有者，添加至少一种语言，选择基本语言并单击保存。 您的项目将准备就绪！

获取示例项目

如果您只是尝试 Tolgee，请阅读本节。 如果你已经有一个项目，你可以跳过这个。

  • 单击此处下载简单的 HTML 和 CSS 模板。

  • 在浏览器中查看 HTML 文件。 模板应如下所示。

  • 目前，该网站仅提供英文版本。 让我们使用 Tolgee 添加翻译。

与 tolgee 集成

让我们首先为我们的集成创建一个 API 密钥。

  • 导航到 Tolgee 中的API 密钥仪表板。
  • 要生成新的 API 密钥，请单击底部的+按钮。

  • 您可以在此处选择多个范围。 范围可帮助您更有效地管理集成。 本文包含有关管理 API 密钥的更多信息。 暂时，选择所有并单击“保存”。

将 tolgee 集成到您的项目中。

  • 转到您的 tolgee 项目的“集成”页面。

  • 如果您从以前共享的链接中获得模板，请选择“Web”，否则，请选择您的项目构建时使用的任何框架。 然后，选择您新创建的 API 密钥。

  • 现在启动任何 IDE 并打开您的项目。
  • 在项目的根目录中创建一个名为index.js的文件。 此文件包含 tolgee 集成代码。
  • 打开index.html文件并将以下标签放在关闭的“body”标签上方，然后再保存您的修改。
 <script src= "https://unpkg.com/@tolgee/core/dist/tolgee.umd.js" ></script> <script src= "https://unpkg.com/@tolgee/ui/dist/tolgee-ui.umd.js" ></script> <script src= "index.js" ></script>
  • 打开index.js文件并粘贴以下代码，将您的 API KEY替换为您的 API 密钥，然后保存修改。
 // index.js const { Tolgee , IcuFormatter } = window [ " @tolgee/core " ]; const tg = Tolgee . use ( IcuFormatter ). init ({ apiUrl : " https://app.tolgee.io " , // ############################################################ // ## you should never leak your API key ## // ## remove it in for production publicly accessible site ## // ############################################################ apiKey : " YOUR API KEY " , // in production mode this is where your // exported translations are loaded from filesUrlPrefix : " /i18n/ " , defaultLanguage : " en " , inputPrefix : " {{ " , inputSuffix : " }} " , watch : true , ui : window [ " @tolgee/ui " ]. UI , }); tg . run ();

创建翻译

  • 您可以通过用两个花括号将它们括起来来创建单词的翻译。 例如， {{Hello World}} 。 现在继续将所有要翻译的句子和单词放在花括号中。
  • 现在，转到您的浏览器并刷新页面。 如果您ALT / OPTION + 单击任何单词或句子，将弹出 Tolgee UI，允许您立即修改或添加翻译。 您的 tolgee 项目将保存所有数据。

  • 这是我翻译的网页😍。

  • 您的所有翻译都可以在您的 Tolgee 项目仪表板中找到。 这样您就可以直接从 Tolgee 项目仪表板编辑翻译。

生产应用程序的上下文翻译

先决条件

  • 一个集成了 Tolgee SDK 的生产 Web 应用程序。
  • 托尔吉扩展

    安装 Tolgee Tools chrome 扩展。 安装后，您可以通过单击 tolgee 图标访问扩展程序。

    使用扩展

    • 启动您的实时网站。
    • 启动 Tolgee 扩展。
    • 提供，
      • 现在ALT / OPTION + 单击任何字符串以打开 Tolgee UI。
      • 在 UI 中，添加或编辑翻译。

      而已！ 托尔吉很棒，对吧？

      在评论中分享您对 Tolgee 的看法👇。

      你觉得这有用吗？ 关注更多🎉。

卡兰·普拉塔普·辛格—— 一旦暂停，itsrakesh 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，itsrakesh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，itsrakesh 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 itsrakesh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 itsrakesh 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 itsrakesh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Developers have had a lot of trouble with localization. It can be difficult at times to create and manage localization strings, deal with external libraries, and more. What about having a one-stop shop for managing everything and making translation work easier?

Let's get started

I often write about amazing Open Source projects. Today I'd like to introduce you to Tolgee, another fantastic Open Source tool. Tolgee is a one-stop shop for project localization. Tolgee eliminates the requirement to choose an i18n library, editor, and platform. Everything you need to localize your project is included. Let's look at how you may get the most out of Tolgee in this article.

Creating a project in Tolgee

Step 1:

  • Open the Tolgee app. You can join using your email and password, or you can immediately authenticate your account through Github.

Step- 2:

  • You will then be able to see the projects dashboard; click on + ADD located at the top-right corner of that dashboard.

Step-3:

  • Name your project, choose the owner, add at least one language, choose base language and click save. Your project will be ready!

Get a sample project

Read this section if you are just trying out Tolgee. If you already have a project, you can skip this.

  • Download a simple HTML and CSS template by clicking here.

  • View the HTML file in your browser. The template should look like this.

  • At the moment, this website is only available in English. Let's use Tolgee to add translations.

Integrating with tolgee

Let's start by creating an API key for our integration.

  • Navigate to the API keys dashboard in Tolgee.
  • To generate a new API key, click the + button at the bottom.

  • You can choose between many scopes here. Scopes help you manage your integration more effectively. This article contains more information about managing API keys. For the time being, pick all and click "Save."

Integrate tolgee in your project.

  • Go to the "Integrate" page of your tolgee project.

  • If you obtained the template from a previously shared link, select "Web," otherwise, select whichever framework your project is built with. Then, choose your freshly created API key.

  • Now launch any IDE and open your project.
  • Make a file called index.js in the project's root directory. This file contains the tolgee integration code.
  • Open the index.html file and put the following tags above the closing 'body' tag before saving your modifications. 
<script src="https://unpkg.com/@tolgee/core/dist/tolgee.umd.js"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/@tolgee/ui/dist/tolgee-ui.umd.js"></script>
<script src="index.js"></script>
  • Open the index.js file and paste the following code, replacing YOUR API KEY with your API key, and then save the modifications. 
// index.js
const { Tolgee, IcuFormatter } = window["@tolgee/core"];
const tg = Tolgee.use(IcuFormatter).init({ apiUrl: "https://app.tolgee.io", // ############################################################ // ## you should never leak your API key ## // ## remove it in for production publicly accessible site ## // ############################################################ apiKey: "YOUR API KEY", // in production mode this is where your // exported translations are loaded from filesUrlPrefix: "/i18n/", defaultLanguage: "en", inputPrefix: "{{", inputSuffix: "}}", watch: true, ui: window["@tolgee/ui"].UI,
});
tg.run();

Create translations

  • You can create translations for words by surrounding them with two curly braces. For example, {{Hello World}}. Now go ahead and put all the sentences and words that you want to translate in curly braces.
  • Now, go to your browser and refresh the page. If you ALT/OPTION + click on any word or sentence, the Tolgee UI will pop up, allowing you to modify or add translations immediately. Your tolgee project will save all data.

  • Here is my translated web page 😍.

  • All of your translations can be found in your Tolgee project dashboard. So that you can edit translations directly from your Tolgee project dashboard.

In-context translation on production application

Tolgee provides an opportunity to add/edit translations to a production application with the in-context feature.

Prerequisites

  • A production web application that integrates the Tolgee SDK.
  • Tolgee account and access to the project.

Tolgee extension

Install Tolgee Tools chrome extension. After installing you can access the extension by clicking on the tolgee icon.

Using extension

  • Start your live website.
  • Launch the Tolgee extension.
  • Provide,
    • API url - Self-hosted Tolgee URL or, if you are using Tolgee platform, then fill https://app.tolgee.io
    • API key - The API key of your Tolgee project.
  • Now ALT / OPTION + click on any string to open Tolgee UI.
  • In the UI, add or edit translations.

That's it! Tolgee is amazing, right?

Share your opinion on Tolgee in the comments 👇.

Did you find this helpful? Follow for more 🎉.

Karan Pratap Singh - Jul 20

Once unsuspended, itsrakesh will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by itsrakesh will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

item.title: Kubernetes 的历史⏱️ | The History of Kubernetes⏱️
item.description:
要开始我们的Kubernetes 历史，我们必须从Google开始。 谷歌在2000 年代初面临一个重大问题。 尽管他们继续主导着数字世界的大部分地区，但他们仍在研究如何从大量免费服务中获得收入。 这包括受欢迎的服务，例如：

  1. 谷歌搜索
  2. 邮箱

谷歌的问题是，它不是我们今天所知道的盈利机器，它正在寻找一种方法来提供这些免费服务，而不会使业务陷入困境。 请记住，要使此类服务保持正常运行需要花费大量成本——尤其是在它们很受欢迎的情况下。

对于还不是盈利机器的谷歌来说，困难在于如何在不破坏业务的情况下提供这些免费服务。 请记住，维护这样的服务是昂贵的，尤其是如果它们很受欢迎的话。

虽然谷歌没有像今天这样产生大量资金，但它确实有一个优势。 你看，即使在千禧年初，谷歌仍然在为其团队吸引最优秀和最聪明的人。 考虑到这一点，谷歌的主要目标发生了变化。
谷歌问它的团队，“我们如何才能从我们的硬件中获得最大的性能？ 进入博格项目（2003-2004）

问题在于管理 Google 庞大的商品硬件架构。 谷歌使用了一种完全不同的方法来实现其新目标。 结果，在 2003-2004 年左右，Google 创建了一个新的应用程序编排系统，称为“ Borg ”。

你可能对 Borg 不熟悉； 名字来源于美国科幻系列星际迷航。 博格人是一个外星群体，在虚构的星际迷航宇宙中作为反复出现的敌人出现。 他们不是好人，但这是最终成为Kubernetes的技术的绝佳比喻。

它甚至早于控制组等 Linux 内核功能，后者是当今容器化应用程序的基础元素。 这意味着该项目对外部派对不太友好，只能在 Google 内部使用。

谷歌当时认为 Borg 为他们提供了竞争优势。 结果，该倡议被保密了。 尽管如此，该项目对现在被称为 Kubernetes 的贡献是无价的。

现在，另一家科技初创公司正在为应用程序开发开发真正尖端的技术。 我们正在谈论Docker 。

我们可以整天谈论Docker 引擎和容器化应用程序。 然而，今天这篇文章的重点是 Docker 对谷歌团队的影响，以及它如何推动一些谷歌员工创建我们今天使用的开源 Kubernetes。

现在，自 Docker 普及容器管理以来， 2013年是有趣的一年，此时 Borg 大约有十年的历史。
这些年来，该项目取得了长足的发展。 事实上，许多团队成员不知道他们的工作在未来会有多大的影响力。
博格此时大约十岁。 然而， Joe Beda、Brendan Burns 和 Craig McLuckie看到了 Docker 容器的流行，并看到了 Borg 一些更有用元素的前进道路。

这三位 Google 员工着手开发一个项目，将 Borg/Omega 的所有出色品质与 Docker 的容器结合起来。 将整个东西开源是一个艰难的选择。 谷歌的云服务当时还处于起步阶段。 这意味着采用仅限 Google 的容器编排平台不是一种选择。

因此， Kubernetes 诞生了，我们今天所知道的 Kubernetes 架构的基本组件成为了科技世界的一部分。

谷歌在2016 年将 Kubernetes 项目交给了云原生计算基金会。 该社区的目的是创建一个供应商中立产品的开源生态系统。
  • 在 1.18 版本之前，Kubernetes 遵循 N-2 支持政策，这意味着最近的三个次要版本会收到安全更新和错误修复。 从 1.19 版开始，Kubernetes 遵循 N-3 支持政策。

    • 我们很高兴看到 Kubernetes 将带我们走向何方。 现在有很多关于“无服务器”解决方案的热议，但Kubernetes正朝着相反的方向发展。 另一方面，Kubernetes 在我们“越来越无服务器”的世界中占有一席之地。 下一步是什么？

    了解 Kubernetes 的历史很有趣，但关于我们最喜欢的编排引擎还有很多需要了解。 查看 -为初学者学习 Kubernetes

    感谢您阅读此博客； 请关注我以获得更多信息！


    原文:

    Kubernetes can speed up the development process by making easy, automated deployments, updates (rolling-update) and by managing our apps and services with almost zero downtime.

    To begin our Kubernetes history, we must begin with Google. Google faced a major issue in the early 2000s. While they continued to dominate the majority of the digital world, they were still figuring out how to make revenue from their extensive number of free services. This included popular services like:

    1. Google Search
    2. YouTube
    3. Gmail

    The problem for Google, which was not the profit-making machine we know today, was finding a way to offer these free services without tanking the business. Keep in mind that it costs a ton to keep services like these up and running — especially if they are popular, which they were.

    The difficulty for Google, which was not yet a profit-making machine, was figuring out how to deliver these free services without destroying the business. Keep in mind that maintaining services like this is expensive, especially if they are popular, as they were.

    While Google was not producing the amount of money that it is today, it did have one advantage. Google, you see, was still pulling the best and brightest to its teams even in the early millennium. With this in mind, Google's primary objective evolved.
    Google asked its team, “How can we get maximum performance from our hardware?”

    Enters the Borg Project(2003-2004)

    The problem was managing Google's huge architecture of commodity hardware. Google used a completely different approach to achieve its new aim. Google's infrastructure and application architecture have to be totally redesigned. As a result, around 2003-2004 Google created a new application orchestration system known as 'Borg'.

    You may be unfamiliar with the Borg; the name comes from the American science fiction series Star Trek. The Borg are an alien group that appears as recurrent enemies in the fictional Star Trek universe. They weren't the good guys, but it's an excellent metaphor for the technology that would eventually become Kubernetes.

    Borg developed the cluster management system required by Google to keep up with the millions of users of its services. It even preceded Linux kernel capabilities like control groups, which are today foundational elements of containerized applications. This meant that the project wasn't very outside party-friendly and could only be used within Google.

    Google felt Borg provided them a competitive advantage at the time. As a result, the initiative was kept a secret. Nonetheless, the project's contributions to what is now known as Kubernetes are priceless.

    The birth of Kubernetes

    Now another tech startup was developing really cutting-edge techniques to application development. We're talking about Docker.

    We could talk about the Docker Engine and containerized apps all day. However, the focus of today's article is on the influence Docker had on Google's team, and how it pushed a few Googlers to create the open-source Kubernetes we use today.

    Now, 2013 was an interesting year since Docker popularised container management, Borg was around a decade old at this point.
    The project had developed significantly throughout those years. The creation and design of Borg and its successor, Omega, taught Google's engineers a lot. In fact, many team members had no idea how influential their work would be in the future.
    Borg was around a decade old at this point. However, Joe Beda, Brendan Burns, and Craig McLuckie looked at the popularity of Docker’s containers and saw a path forward for some of Borg’s more useful elements.

    Those three Googlers set out to develop a project that would combine all of the fantastic qualities of Borg/Omega with Docker's containers. It was a difficult choice to make the entire thing open source. Google's cloud services were in their beginnings at the time. This meant that adopting a Google-only container orchestration platform wasn't an option.

    Google Cloud was able to create an useable and scalable containerized orchestration platform internally with the Borg/Omega project, but to bridge the gap between internal and external required the participation of engineers all around the world — and the only way to do this was to make the whole project open-source.

    Thus, Kubernetes was born, and the basic components of Kubernetes architecture that we know today became part of the tech world.

    Key events after 2014:

    • Kubernetes v1.0.0 was released on July of 2015, since than we’ve only seen the Kubernetes cluster grow.
    • Google worked with the Linux Foundation to form the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in 2015.
    • Google gave the Kubernetes project to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation in 2016. (CNCF). The CNCF was established in 2015 as a Linux Foundation initiative and presently has over 500 members, including developers, end users, and IT technology and service providers. This community's purpose is to create an open source ecosystem of vendor-neutral products.
    • In February 2016, the Helm package manager for Kubernetes was released. Helm basically helps you manage Kubernetes applications.
    • On March 6, 2018, Kubernetes Project reached ninth place in the list of GitHub projects by the number of commits, and second place in authors and issues, after the Linux kernel.
    • Until version 1.18, Kubernetes followed an N-2 support policy, meaning that the three most recent minor versions receive security updates and bug fixes. Starting with version 1.19, Kubernetes follows an N-3 support policy.

    Future of Kubernetes

    We are excited to see where Kubernetes will take us. There is a lot of buzz these days about 'serverless' solutions, but Kubernetes is headed in the opposite way. Kubernetes, on the other hand, has a place in our 'increasingly serverless' world. Kubernetes-based tools such as Kubeless and Fission provide equivalents to functions-as-a-service.

    What’s next?

    It was interesting to learn about the history of Kubernetes, but there is a lot more to know about our favourite orchestration engine. Check out - Learn Kubernetes for Beginners

    Thank you for reading this blog; do follow me for more!
item.author: iarchitsharma

item.title: 您缺少的 Chrome 开发工具：FEAT Recorder | The Chrome Dev Tool you are missing : FEAT Recorder
item.description:
有没有在开发过程中重复手动测试某些东西的时候？

例如表单验证、填写表单和用户流程。

这个博客是为谁写的？
这适用于愿意加倍努力以自动化测试其流程的开发人员。 例子
让我们假设这个布局怪异的表单，其中Next按钮被禁用，直到所有详细信息都被填写。 我们正在进行第二步，我们希望单击下一步并测试一些东西。

想象一下手动填充它超过 20 次！ 这就是 chrome 记录器的用武之地😇

这就是录音机的作用。 这是我第一次训练录音机做我正在做的事情，从下一次开始，我们可以让录音机控制它。

让我们看看我们如何做到这一点

  1. 打开你的控制台/开发者工具
  2. 按CMD/CTRL + SHIFT + P打开命令运行
  3. 键入记录器并单击显示记录器
  4. 单击开始新录制并执行流程。 而已

录制完成后有很多选项。

您可以导出为 json 并共享。

可以编辑所有事件，例如值更改和其他内容。 您还可以添加超时来为 api 调用让路。

有关更多信息，请访问记录、回放和测量用户流

我已经在 Angular 11 和 Nextjs 中测试了这个特性。 可以肯定地说，在测试流程时，我在公司节省了时间（很多无聊的东西）。

平安🕊

如果您在这里，则意味着您可能喜欢阅读此博客。 只要关注我@shrihari ，这将激励我写更多东西，贡献开源。

你可以让我喝酪乳🥛 。 小小支持大有来头！

订阅 如果您想从@Medium 的邮件中免费接收这些博客！

我的更多免费文章

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— 一旦暂停，shrihari 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，shrihari 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，shrihari 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 shrihari 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 shrihari 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 shrihari 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

There ever been the time where you would repeatedly testing something manually when you are developing?

For example Form Validations , Filling up the forms and user flows.

Who is this blog for ?
This is meant for developers who are willing to go extra mile for testing their flow with automation. (This feature is still experimental)

Example
Lets assume this wierdly layed out form where the Next button is disabled until all the details are filled. We're working on the 2nd step where we would want to click next and test something.

Imagine going over 20 times manually filling this up! That is where chrome recorder comes in 😇

This is what recorder does. For the very first time I train the recorder do what I am doing and from next time onwards we can just let the recorder take control doing that.

Lets see how we can do this

  1. Open your console / Developer tools
  2. Press CMD/CTRL + SHIFT + P to open command run
  3. Type Recorder and click Show Recorder
  4. Click Start new recording and do the flow. Thats it

There are lot of options after you have done recording.

You can export as json and share.

All the events can be edited such as value changes and other stuff. You can also add timeouts to make way for api calls.

For more information visit Record, replay, and measure user flows

I have tested this feature in Angular 11 and Nextjs. Its safe to say I've saved time ( lot of boring stuff ) at my company when testing flows.

Peace 🕊

If you are here it means you may have enjoyed reading this blog. Just follow me @shrihari which will motivate to write more, contribute open source.

You can make me drink Buttermilk 🥛. Small support comes a long way!

Subscribe If you want to receive these blogs in your mail from @Medium for free!

More Free Articles From me

item.title: 并发与并行 | Concurrency vs Parallelism
item.description:
在manning.com 结帐时将 fccbobrov输入折扣代码框，即可享受Grokking Concurrency的 25% 折扣。

区分并行和并发编程非常重要，因为两者在不同的概念级别上追求不同的目标。

并发

并发是关于在重叠的时间段内开始、运行和完成的多个任务，没有特定的顺序。 并行性是关于在具有多个计算资源（如多核处理器）的硬件上同时运行的多个任务或同一任务的子任务。 如您所见，并发性和并行性是相似的，但并不完全相同。

并发是程序或系统的语义属性。 并发是指多个任务在重叠的时间段内进行。 注意，这里我们不是在讨论任务的实际执行，而是系统的设计——任务是顺序无关的。 因此，并发是程序或系统的概念属性，它更多的是关于程序或系统的设计方式。

想象一下，一位厨师正在切沙拉，同时偶尔在炉子上搅拌汤。 他必须停止切菜，检查炉顶，然后再次开始切菜，重复这个过程，直到一切都完成。

可以看到，我们这里只有一个处理资源，厨师，他的并发主要是和物流有关； 没有并发，厨师必须等到炉子上的汤准备好切沙拉。

并行性

并行性是一种实现属性。 并行性实际上是在运行时同时物理执行任务，它需要具有多种计算资源的硬件。 它位于硬件层。

回到厨房，现在我们有两位厨师，一位会搅拌，一位会切沙拉。 我们通过另一个处理资源，另一个厨师来划分工作。

并行是并发的一个子类：在你可以一次执行多个任务之前，你必须先管理多个任务。

并发和并行关系的本质是并发计算可以在不改变结果正确性的情况下并行化，但并发本身并不意味着并行。 此外，并行性并不意味着并发。 优化器通常可以采用没有语义并发的程序，并通过管道处理、宽向量 SIMD 操作或分而治之等技术将它们分解为并行组件。

正如 Rob Pike 指出的那样， “并发性就是一次处理很多事情。 并行就是一次做很多事情。” 在单核 CPU 中，您可以具有并发性，但不能具有并行性。 但两者都超越了传统的顺序模型，在这种模型中，事情一次发生一件。

要进一步了解并发性和并行性之间的区别，请考虑以下几点：

  • 一个应用程序可以是并发的，但不是并行的，这意味着它同时处理多个任务，但没有两个任务同时执行。
  • 应用程序可以并行但不能并发，这意味着它同时处理单个任务的多个子任务。
  • 应用程序既不能并行也不能并发，这意味着它一次按顺序处理一个任务，并且该任务永远不会分解为子任务。
  • 一个应用程序既可以是并行的，也可以是并发的，这意味着它同时处理多个任务或单个任务的子任务（并行执行它们）

我不想成为那个人，但术语很重要。 经常发生的情况是，关于问题的对话会变得混乱，因为一个人想到了并发，而另一个人想到了并行性。 在实践中，并发和并行之间的区别并不是绝对的。 许多程序都有各自的方面。

想象一下，您有一个将值插入哈希表的程序。 如果将插入操作分布在多个内核之间，那就是并行性。 但是协调对哈希表的访问是并发的。

这就是本文的全部内容。 谢谢阅读。 查看Grokking Concurrency了解更多信息！

最初发表于https://freecontent.manning.com



原文:

An excerpt from Grokking Concurrency by Kirill Bobrov

Take 25% off Grokking Concurrency by entering fccbobrov into the discount code box at checkout at manning.com.

The conversational meanings of the words “parallel” and “concurrent” are mostly synonymous, which is a source of significant confusion that extends even to the computer science literature.  Distinguishing between parallel and concurrent programming is very important because both pursue different goals at different conceptual levels.

Concurrency

Concurrency is about multiple tasks which start, run, and complete in overlapping time periods, in no specific order. Parallelism is about multiple tasks or subtasks of the same task that literally run at the same time on a hardware with multiple computing resources like multi-core processor. As you can see, concurrency and parallelism are similar but not identical.

Concurrency is a semantic property of a program or system. Concurrency is when multiple tasks are in progress for overlapping periods of time. Note, here we are not talking about the actual execution of the tasks, but the design of the system – that the tasks are order-independent. So, concurrency is a conceptual property of a program or a system, it’s more about how the program or system has been designed.

Imagine that one cook is chopping salad while occasionally stirring the soup on the stove. He has to stop chopping, check the stove top, and then start chopping again, and repeat this process until everything is done.

As you can see, we only have one processing resource here, the chef, and his concurrency is mostly related to logistics; without concurrency, the chef has to wait until the soup on the stove is ready to chop the salad.

Parallelism

Parallelism is an implementation property. Parallelism is literally the simultaneous physical execution of tasks at runtime, and it requires hardware with multiple computing resources. It resides on the hardware layer.

Back in the kitchen, now we have two chefs, one who can do stirring and one who can chop the salad. We’ve divided the work by having another processing resource, another chef.

Parallelism is a subclass of concurrency: before you can do several tasks at once, you have to manage several tasks first.

The essence of the relationship between concurrency and parallelism is that concurrent computations can be parallelized without changing the correctness of the result, but concurrency itself does not imply parallelism. Furthermore, parallelism does not imply concurrency; it is often possible for an optimizer to take programs with no semantic concurrency and break them down into parallel components via such techniques as pipeline processing, wide vector SIMD operations, or divide and conquer.

As Rob Pike pointed out, “Concurrency is about dealing with lots of things at once. Parallelism is about doing lots of things at once.” In a single-core CPU, you can have concurrency but not parallelism. But both go beyond the traditional sequential model in which things happen one at a time.

To get more idea about the distinction between concurrency and parallelism, consider the following points:

  • An application can be concurrent but not parallel, which means that it processes more than one task at the same time, but no two tasks are executing at the same time instant.
  • An application can be parallel but not concurrent, which means that it processes multiple sub-tasks of a single task at the same time.
  • An application can be neither parallel nor concurrent, which means that it processes one task at a time, sequentially, and the task is never broken into subtasks.
  • An application can be both parallel and concurrent, which means that it processes multiple tasks or subtasks of a single task concurrently at the same time (executing them in parallel)

I don’t want to be that guy, but terminology is important. Too often it happens that the conversation about the problem gets confusing because one person thinks of concurrency and the other thinks of parallelism. In practice, the distinction between concurrency and parallelism is not absolute. Many programs have aspects of each.

Imagine you have a program that inserts values into a hash table. If you spread the insert operation between multiple cores, that’s parallelism. But coordinating access to the hash table is concurrency.

That’s all for this article. Thanks for reading. Check out Grokking Concurrency for more!

Originally published at https://freecontent.manning.com

item.title: 每周摘要 30/2022 | Weekly Digest 30/2022
item.description:
马可·比德曼

发表于欢迎来到我的每周文摘#30。

这个每周摘要包含许多有趣和鼓舞人心的文章、视频、推文、播客和我在这周使用的设计。

有趣的文章阅读

使用容器查询和:has()解决“The Dangler”难题

了解 JavaScript 最基本的事情之一是程序由语句组成，并且语句具有用于表达式的槽。 在这篇博文中，我们将深入研究这两种结构的工作原理，并了解建立对此的直觉如何帮助我们解决实际问题。

由Google Chrome 开发者提供

我按了⌘B。 你不会相信接下来发生的事情

每当您在 Figma 中按 ⌘B 时，都会发生令人惊讶的复杂事件的编排。 这是两个世界的碰撞——键盘快捷键和排版——每个都有数百年的历史。

通过Marcin Wichary

在本集中，Bramus 和 Jecelyn 将向您介绍在控制台中记录和过滤消息的不同方式。

由Google Chrome 开发者提供

有用的 GitHub 存储库

魔术正则表达式

一种编译后的、类型安全的、可读的 RegExp 替代方案

  • 变更日志
  • 文档
  • 在线游乐场

特征

  • 运行时是零依赖和超最小的
  • 附带转换以编译为纯正则表达式
  • 自动键入的捕获组

阅读更多

扑通扑通

一致性变得简单

微型生成器框架，可让整个团队轻松创建具有一定一致性的文件

入门

扑通扑通是什么？

现在，我之所以这么称呼它，是因为它是一个小工具，可以让您以一种简单的方式以一致的方式生成代码或任何其他类型的平面文本文件。 你看，我们都在代码中创建结构和模式（路由、控制器、组件、助手等）。 这些模式会随着时间的推移而变化和改进，因此当您需要在此处创建新的 insert-name-of-pattern-here时，在代码库中找到代表当前“最佳实践”的文件并不总是那么容易。 这就是扑通救你的地方。 有了 plop，您就有了在 CODE 中创建任何给定模式的“最佳实践”方法。 可以从终端轻松运行的代码……

胶

迷人的 shell 脚本工具

用于迷人的 shell 脚本的工具。 在您的脚本和别名中利用Bubbles和Lip Gloss的强大功能，而无需编写任何 Go 代码！

上面的示例是从单个 shell 脚本 ( source ) 运行的。

教程

让我们构建一个简单的脚本来帮助您为您的点文件编写常规提交。

以#!/bin/sh开头。

使用gum choose询问提交类型： 

gum choose " fix " " feat " " docs " " style " " refactor " " test " " chore " " revert "

提示：此命令本身将打印到stdout ，这并不是那么有用 要稍后使用该命令，您可以将标准输出保存到$VARIABLE或file.txt ...

由卡夫特

通过哈比比

农业助理应用程序

由概念车

推文

我觉得我连续 5 天看到了一个狂野而复杂的 CSS 选择器。

今天的版本——你还记得数量查询吗，它只允许对有 X 个元素进行样式设置。 想在控制台中查看当前文档中的所有 ID？
每次我发现 zsh/oh-my-zsh 在 TAB 上有自动路径扩展时，我都很惊讶。 但它不知何故不坚持。 也许这一次！

值得一听的播客

在这篇 Hasty Treat 中，Scott 和 Wes 讨论了 Bun，一个新的一体化 JavaScript 运行时。 是什么让包子这么快？ 为什么我们需要另一个 JavaScript 运行时？

原文:

Marco Biedermann

Posted on Jul 31

Welcome to my Weekly Digest #30.

This weekly digest contains a lot of interesting and inspiring articles, videos, tweets, podcasts, and designs I consumed during this week.

Interesting articles to read

Solving “The Dangler” Conundrum with Container Queries and :has()

Solving "The Dangler" Conundrum with Container Queries and :has()

Statements Vs. Expressions

One of the most foundational things to understand about JavaScript is that programs are made up of statements, and statements have slots for expressions. In this blog post, we'll dig into how these two structures work, and see how building an intuition about this can help us solve practical problems.

Statements Vs. Expressions

Some great videos I watched this week

Thinking on ways to solve carousels

In today's GUI Challenge, @Adam Argyle shares the features and aspects of a carousel component: adaptive theming, adaptive to various user input types, adaptive to author time options, and adaptive to user preferences.

by Google Chrome Developers

Chrome 104 - What’s New in DevTools

Restart frame during debugging, slow replay options in the Recorder panel, and more.

by Google Chrome Developers

I pressed ⌘B. You wouldn’t believe what happened next

Whenever you press ⌘B in Figma, what happens is a choreography of events of surprising complexity. It’s a collision of two worlds – keyboard shortcuts and typography – each one with hundreds of years of history.

by Marcin Wichary

How to log messages in the Console | DevTools Tips

In this episode, Bramus and Jecelyn walk you through the different ways of logging and filtering messages in the Console.

by Google Chrome Developers

Useful GitHub repositories

magic-regexp

A compiled-away, type-safe, readable RegExp alternative

A compiled-away, type-safe, readable RegExp alternative

  • ✨  Changelog
  • 📖  Documentation
  • ▶️  Online playground

Features

  • Runtime is zero-dependency and ultra-minimal
  • Ships with transform to compile to pure RegExp
  • Automatically typed capture groups
  • Natural language syntax
  • Generated RegExp displays on hover

📖  Read more

💻 Development

  • Clone this repository
  • Enable Corepack using corepack enable (use npm i -g corepack for Node.js < 16.10)
  • Install dependencies using pnpm install
  • Run interactive tests using pnpm dev

Similar packages

  • verbal-expressions
  • typed-regex

License

Made with ❤️

Published under MIT License.




Plop

Consistency Made Simple

Micro-generator framework that makes it easy for an entire team to create files with a level of uniformity

Documentation also available on plopjs.com

Getting Started

   

What is Plop?

Plop is what I like to call a "micro-generator framework." Now, I call it that because it is a small tool that gives you a simple way to generate code or any other type of flat text files in a consistent way. You see, we all create structures and patterns in our code (routes, controllers, components, helpers, etc). These patterns change and improve over time so when you need to create a NEW insert-name-of-pattern-here, it's not always easy to locate the files in your codebase that represent the current "best practice." That's where plop saves you. With plop, you have your "best practice" method of creating any given pattern in CODE. Code that can easily be run from the terminal…

Gum

A tool for glamorous shell scripts

A tool for glamorous shell scripts. Leverage the power of Bubbles and Lip Gloss in your scripts and aliases without writing any Go code!

The above example is running from a single shell script (source).

Tutorial

Gum provides highly configurable, ready-to-use utilities to help you write useful shell scripts and dotfiles aliases with just a few lines of code.

Let's build a simple script to help you write Conventional Commits for your dotfiles.

Start with a #!/bin/sh.

Ask for the commit type with gum choose:

gum choose "fix" "feat" "docs" "style" "refactor" "test" "chore" "revert"

Tip: this command itself will print to stdout which is not all that useful To make use of the command later on you can save the stdout to a $VARIABLE or file.txt…

dribbble shots

Cyberpunk Dystopia 3D Icon Set

by Krafted

Food Delivery Maemlur - Mobile Apps.

by Habibi

Agriculture Assistant App

by Conceptzilla

Tweets

I feel like I saw a wild and complex CSS selector the last 5 days in a row.

Today's edition – do you remember the quantity query, which allows to style elements only if there's X of them. 😲🙈

Want to see all IDs in the current document in the Console?
console.table($$('*[id]'),['tagName','id'])
Every time I discover that zsh/oh-my-zsh has auto path expansion on TAB, I'm so amazed. But it somehow doesn't stick. Maybe this time!

Video alt: terminal command "cd p/g/s/d" which expands to "cd Projects/github.com/stefanjudis/dotfiles" after pressing tab.

Picked Pens

Responsive Faux 3D Content Scroller

by Jhey

CSS only back to top button

by Temani Afif

Cascading CSS Text Effects

by Jhey

Podcasts worth listening

Syntax – What is Bun? The New JS Runtime

In this Hasty Treat, Scott and Wes talk about Bun, a new all-in-one JavaScript runtime. What makes Bun so fast? What's on Bun's roadmap? And why do we need another JavaScript runtime?

Thank you for reading, talk to you next week, and stay safe! 👋

Dreamy Developer - Jul 27

item.title: Astro 和向 MDX 的迁移 | Astro and the move to MDX
item.description:
我是个头发花白的老家伙，我在 1975 年看过原版的“万岁玛丽”剧，所以当我看到一个时，我喜欢认为我知道一个好的“万岁玛丽”。 事实上，我可能就是这样做的。

四月，我写了《 Astro 准备好你的博客了吗？ 当您编辑 Markdown 或.astro文件时，较大的网站不会很快刷新所有内容。 在刷新开发模式的速度方面，所有其他竞争对手目前在该项目上都在 Astro 上具有优势，速度惊人的Hugo显然处于领先地位。

这就是我的意思。 当我在开发模式下运行 Hugo 并将更改保存到我网站的任何“观察”文件（内容、样式、布局等）时，我几乎可以立即在屏幕上看到结果； 但是，对于当时的 Astro，这样的行为导致 Astro 在显示修订之前“思考”了几秒钟。

在随后的“考虑迁移？” 发布后，我添加了更多详细信息：

如果您当前的站点只有几页，您会发现 Astro 的开发服务器会以足够快的速度刷新浏览器以适合您。 但是，在您的内容达到或超过 100 页级别后，即使是相当简单的内容编辑也会导致在您保存正在更改的文件时刷新需要几秒钟，并且随着站点的增加延迟会变长变大。

因此，对于较大的站点，您可能希望习惯于在开发模式之外编写内容，然后才激活 Astro 开发服务器以检查新页面的外观。

这种开发模式的迟缓并不是主要原因，正如我在那篇文章末尾写的那样，在使用新的 SSG 几周后，我在 5 月将我的网站从 Astro 移回了 Hugo，但它没有没有帮助。 一路走来，我看到原定于 6 月初推出的 Astro 1.0 已经多次下滑。 到目前为止，假设当前的候选版本 (RC) 就足够了，它将在下周进行。 这篇文章是关于在开发过程中很晚才做出的一个关键决定：Astro 正在将内容管理支持从 Markdown 转移到MDX 。 至于为什么 Astro 团队做出这样的决定，尤其是离“发货”v.1.0 的预期日期如此之近，Astro 联合​​创始人 Fred K. Schott 最近对 Astro Discord 的评论可能特别能说明问题。 当用户想知道在 Astro-flavored Markdown 中使用组件的问题时，Schott 回答说：

是的，你已经明白了我们花了一年时间学习的东西：构建你自己的 MD [plus] 组件系统和语法真的很困难[。] 😅 我们现在正在试验 MDX [Markdown;] 中组件的解决方案，而不是我们必须开发自己的系统，我们可以利用 MDX 来代替。 我们已经有了实验支持。 但如果一切顺利，这将及时成为 v1.0 的标准，因为我们会优先考虑我们自己的 [.]

这有点让人想起去年年底 Astro 开发人员决定将 Astro 从他们自己的Snowpack平台过渡到 Vite。 这并不容易，但性能上的胜利意义重大。

以类似的方式，在球赛后期从 Markdown 迁移到 MDX 是一场豪赌。 然而，Astro 开发人员在尝试让 Markdown 和 Astro 很好地结合在一起时遇到了麻烦，这是有道理的——特别是考虑到 Astro 存在的理由有多少不仅涉及令人愉快的 DX，而且还涉及标记和组件之间的平滑交互。

到这个周末，Astro 团队似乎让 MDX 在最新的 RC 版本上运行良好，所以我对其进行了一些测试。 具体来说，我想查看一个 Astro 站点的 DX，它的 MDX 文件与我当前的 Hugo 站点的 Markdown 文件一样多。 事实上，我什至通过为测试项目提供更多这样的文件来将甲板与 Astro 相提并论。 根据我的测试，迁移到 MDX 看起来像是赢家。 在开发模式下，对“观看”文件的编辑几乎立即显示（可以说，Astro 的 MDX DX 与Eleventy的带有普通 Markdown 的非常相似）。 对于一些拥有大量 Markdown 网站的人来说，这可以简化向 Astro 的迁移，这些人根本无法忍受在 Astro/MDX 交互成为现实之前遇到的类似糖蜜的 DX。

那么这张照片有什么问题呢？ 好吧，任何已经将现有 Markdown 内容从另一个 SSG 转移到 Astro 的人——尤其是考虑到这样的转移所需的变化——现在必须再次这样做。 然而，对于那些还没有做出如此痛苦改变的人来说，将常规 Markdown 文件移动到配备 MDX 的 Astro 网站要容易得多：您只需将它们的扩展名从.md更改为.mdx 。 当然，您必须解决您可能在另一个 SSG 上的 Markdown 中添加的任何“特殊”内容，就像在任何两个 SSG 之间移动一样； 但是，现在，作为.mdx的通用Markdown 可以在 Astro 中正常工作。

与任何其他 SSG（尤其是基于 JavaScript 的 SSG）一样，Astro 的稳定性与其许多第三方依赖项的稳定性一样好； 其中一些在撰写本文时仍然有点不稳定。 也就是说，就 RC 成为真正的v.1.0 的准备情况而言，该团队在这一点上非常接近终点线——而且，在我看来，新的 MDX 兼容性是一个令人惊讶的重要原因。 切换是一个大胆的举动，更不用说在 v.1.0 的准备阶段，但也是一个需要做出的举动。

致 The Astro Technology Company 的好人：我想你们刚刚扔了一条“冰雹玛丽”的鲸鱼，因为第四节的时钟正滴答作响地归零。 希望 v.1.0 是一次成功的达阵。

  1. 对不起，明尼苏达维京队的球迷，但我对你关于进攻性传球干扰比赛的抱怨不感兴趣。 那是很久很久以前的事了。 克服它。 不过，随着时间的推移，我发现了更喜欢 Hugo DX 的其他原因，尤其是当我更深入地研究需要访问远程 API 的编码项目时。 Hugo makes that work considerably easier than does any Node.js -based SSG. Hugo 使这项工作比任何基于Node.js的 SSG 都容易得多。 ↩ ↩



原文:

Grizzled old guy that I am, I saw the original “Hail Mary” play when it happened1 back in 1975, so I like to think I know a good “Hail Mary” when I see one. In fact, I may have done just that.

In April, I wrote “Is Astro ready for your blog?” In it, I noted that, although the Astro static site generator (SSG) was (as it still is) justly receiving a lot of love for its many cool features and the leanness of Astro-built sites, it did suffer somewhat regarding the developer experience on a site containing a large number of Markdown files:

. . . larger sites don’t refresh all that quickly when you edit either Markdown or an .astro file . . . . where refresh-in-dev-mode speed is concerned, all the other competitors currently have an edge on Astro for this item, with the stunningly fast Hugo obviously leading the pack.

(Link added to original text.)

Here's what I meant. When I’m running Hugo in dev mode and save a change to any of my site’s “watched” files (content, style, layout, etc.), I see the result almost instantly on-screen; but, with Astro back then, such actions caused Astro to “think about” the change for several seconds before it would display the revision.

In the subsequent “Mulling over migration?” post, I added more details:

If your current site has only a few pages, you’ll find Astro's dev server refreshes the browser quickly enough to suit you. However, after your content gets up to and beyond about the 100-page level, even a fairly simple content edit will cause the refresh to take several seconds when you save the file you’re changing, and the lag will get longer as the site gets bigger.

The Astro devs are aware of the issue — perhaps related to Astro's interaction with Vite — but it’s unknown when it’ll be fixed. Thus, with a larger site, you may want to get used to writing your content outside of development mode and, only then, activating the Astro dev server to check the new page’s appearance.

This dev-mode sluggishness wasn’t the main reason why, as I wrote near the end of that post, I had moved my site off Astro and back to Hugo in May after only a few weeks with the newer SSG, but it didn’t help.2 Still, I retained interest in and curiosity about Astro, so I continued occasionally to look in on its Discord community.

Along the way, I saw that the launch of Astro 1.0, originally planned for early June, had slipped multiple times. As of now, it’s set for next week, assuming the current Release Candidate (RC) suffices. This post is about one key decision that came very late in the dev process: Astro is shifting content management support from Markdown to MDX. (While Markdown will still work in Astro at least for the time being, it’ll do so under a “legacy” flag.)

As for why the Astro team made such a decision, especially so close to the desired date for “shipping” v.1.0, one recent comment on the Astro Discord by Astro co-creator Fred K. Schott may be especially telling. When a user wondered about problems with using components in Astro-flavored Markdown, Schott replied:

Yeah, you’ve kind of hit [on] the thing it’s taken us a year to learn: it’s really hard to build your own MD [plus] components system and syntax[.] 😅 We’re experimenting now with MDX as a better solution for components in [Markdown;] instead of us having to develop our own system we could leverage MDX instead. We already have experimental support . . . but if all goes well this would become standard in time for v1.0 with us deprioritizing our own[.]

This is somewhat reminiscent of the Astro devs’ decision late last year to transition Astro from their own Snowpack platform to Vite. It wasn’t easy, but the performance win was significant.

In similar fashion, making the move from Markdown to MDX this late in the ballgame is a big gamble. However, with the hassles the Astro devs have encountered while trying to make Markdown and Astro play nicely together, it makes sense — particularly considering how much of Astro’s raison d'être involves not only a pleasant DX but also smooth interaction between markup and components.

By this weekend, the Astro team appeared to have MDX working smoothly with the latest RC version, so I put it through some tests. Specifically, I wanted to see the DX for an Astro site with about as many MDX files as my current Hugo site has Markdown files. In fact, I even stacked the deck a bit against Astro by giving the test project more such files. (Each MDX file had numerous paragraphs of “lorem ipsum” boilerplate, enough to constitute plenty of text for Astro to manage.)

Based on my tests, the move to MDX looks like a winner. In dev mode, edits to “watched” files show up virtually immediately (I’d say Astro's MDX DX, so to speak, is very similar to that of Eleventy’s with plain Markdown). This could simplify a move to Astro for some folks with big, Markdown-heavy sites, folks who simply couldn’t stomach the molasses-like DX that they’d encountered before Astro/MDX interactivity became a thing.

So what could be wrong with this picture? Well, anyone who had already transitioned existing Markdown content from another SSG to Astro — especially given the changes such a move required — now must do so again. However, for those who hadn’t yet made that wrenching change, it’s a lot easier to move regular Markdown files to an MDX-equipped Astro site: you just change their extensions from .md to .mdx. Of course, you’ll have to address any “special” stuff you may have put in that Markdown on the other SSG, just as you would in moving between any two SSGs; but, now, generic Markdown as .mdx will work just fine in Astro.

The stability of Astro, like any other SSG (especially a JavaScript-based one), is only as good as the stability of its many third-party dependencies; and some of those are still a little shaky at this writing. That said, where the RC’s readiness for becoming the real v.1.0 is concerned, the team is pretty near the finish line on this count — and, in my view, the new MDX compatibility is a surprisingly big reason why. The switch was a gutsy move to make at all, much less at this point in the run-up to v.1.0, but also one that needed to be made.

To the good folks at The Astro Technology Company: I think you just threw one whale of a “Hail Mary” as the fourth-quarter clock was ticking down to zero. Here’s hoping v.1.0 is a winning touchdown.

  1. Sorry, Minnesota Vikings fans, but I’m not interested in your complaints about offensive pass interference on the play. It was a long, long time ago. Get over it. (Also: in the famous film sequence of Drew Pearson as he crossed the goal line with the winning score, that was an orange thrown onto the field by a fan, not a penalty flag thrown by a referee. Truth hurts; I know.) ↩

  2. In time, though, I found other reasons to prefer the Hugo DX, especially as I delved more deeply into coding projects requiring access to remote APIs. Hugo makes that work considerably easier than does any Node.js-based SSG. ↩
item.author: brycewray

item.title: 使用 Spotless 标准化代码格式 | Standardize code formatting with Spotless
item.description:
安吉亚达夫

发表于将代码组织和格式化作为整个团队的标准是重要且有用的。 如果项目中没有遵循标准来格式化代码，则会出现以下问题：

  • 从存储库更新期间和合并期间的冲突
  • 开发人员使用他们喜欢的格式
  • 为了建立关于代码格式化的标准，我们需要设置一个通用的配置文件以供每个人导入，或者我们可以使用像 Spotless 这样的东西，它可以配置为使用现有的代码格式化标准，包括 Google Java 格式等。

    在这里，我将解释我们在应用程序中使用的 Spotless 的用法。

    设置非常简单，无论您使用 Maven 还是 Gradle，都只包含两个步骤：

    1. 包括一尘不染的依赖：“com.diffplug.spotless”
    2. 根据您的格式偏好配置插件
    3. 使其成为构建的一部分（可选但首选）

    包括一尘不染的依赖

    马文

    <dependency>   <groupId>com.diffplug.spotless</groupId>   <artifactId>spotless-maven-plugin</artifactId> </dependency>

    摇篮

    dependencies {  implementation("com.diffplug.spotless:spotless-lib:${spotlessLibVersion}")   implementation("com.diffplug.spotless:spotless-plugin-gradle:${spotlessVersion}") }

    配置插件

    马文

    <plugin>  <groupId>com.diffplug.spotless</groupId>  <artifactId>spotless-maven-plugin</artifactId>  <configuration>    <ratchetFrom>origin/develop</ratchetFrom>    <java>      <includes>        <include>src/main/java/**/*.java</include>        <include>src/test/java/**/*.java</include>      </includes>      <importOrder />      <removeUnusedImports />      <toggleOffOn/>      <trimTrailingWhitespace/>      <endWithNewline/>      <indent>        <tabs>true</tabs>        <spacesPerTab>4</spacesPerTab>      </indent>      <palantirJavaFormat/>    </java>  </configuration> </plugin>

    摇篮

    spotless {  java {    target fileTree('.') {      include '**/*.java'      exclude '**/build/**', '**/build-*/**'    }    toggleOffOn()    palantirJavaFormat()    removeUnusedImports()    trimTrailingWhitespace()    endWithNewline()  } }

    上述示例中用于格式化的常用配置

    • 对代码执行一尘不染

      马文
      mvn spotless:apply - To implement automated code formatting
      mvn spotless:check - To validate if code was formatted

      摇篮

      .\gradlew clean build spotlessApply - To implement automated code formatting
      .\gradlew clean build spotlessCheck - To validate if code was formatted​​​​​​​

      参考： Github/spotless

桑吉思·阿鲁拉吉—— 一旦暂停，ankityadav33 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ankityadav33 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ankityadav33 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ankityadav33 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 ankityadav33 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ankityadav33 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ankit Yadav

Posted on Jul 31

Organizing and formatting code as a standard for the whole team is important and useful. Without a standard being followed for formatting code within the project following problems:

  • Conflicts during updates from repository and during merge
  • Developers using their preferred formatting
  • Different auto format settings in IDEA showing more changes as when committing code

In order to setup a standard in regards to code formatting, we need to setup either a common configuration file to be imported by everyone or we can use something like Spotless which could be configured to use existing code formatting standards including Google Java Format and others.

Spotless is available for lots of languages like: Java, Groovy, Kotlin, Scala, C/C++, Python, Sql, Typescript 

Here I am going to explain the usage of Spotless which we are using in our applications.

The setup is really simple and comprises of only two steps no matter you use Maven or Gradle:

  1. Include the spotless dependency: "com.diffplug.spotless"
  2. Configure the plugin based on what your formatting preferences
  3. Make it part of build (optional but preferred)

Include the spotless dependency

Maven

<dependency>
    <groupId>com.diffplug.spotless</groupId>
    <artifactId>spotless-maven-plugin</artifactId>
</dependency>

Gradle

dependencies {
    implementation("com.diffplug.spotless:spotless-lib:${spotlessLibVersion}")
    implementation("com.diffplug.spotless:spotless-plugin-gradle:${spotlessVersion}")
}

Configure the plugin

Maven

<plugin>
    <groupId>com.diffplug.spotless</groupId>
    <artifactId>spotless-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    <configuration>
        <ratchetFrom>origin/develop</ratchetFrom>
        <java>
            <includes>
                <include>src/main/java/**/*.java</include>
                <include>src/test/java/**/*.java</include>
            </includes>
            <importOrder />
            <removeUnusedImports />
            <toggleOffOn/>
            <trimTrailingWhitespace/>
            <endWithNewline/>
            <indent>
                <tabs>true</tabs>
                <spacesPerTab>4</spacesPerTab>
            </indent>
            <palantirJavaFormat/>
        </java>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

Gradle

spotless {
    java {
        target fileTree('.') {
            include '**/*.java'
            exclude '**/build/**', '**/build-*/**'
        }
        toggleOffOn()
        palantirJavaFormat()
        removeUnusedImports()
        trimTrailingWhitespace()
        endWithNewline()
    }
}

Common configurations used in the above example for formatting

  • importOrder- All imports are ordered
  • removeUnusedImports - Unused imports are removed
  • toggleOffOn - toggle formatting in code by a comment
  • trimTrailingWhitespace - All trailing whitespaces in the code are removed
  • endWithNewline - The file ends with a new line

Execute spotless on code

Maven
mvn spotless:apply - To implement automated code formatting
mvn spotless:check - To validate if code was formatted

Gradle

.\gradlew clean build spotlessApply - To implement automated code formatting
.\gradlew clean build spotlessCheck - To validate if code was formatted​​​​​​​

Reference: Github/spotless

Sangeeth Arulraj - Mar 15

Once suspended, ankityadav33 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ankityadav33 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ankityadav33 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ankityadav33 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ankityadav33:

ankityadav33 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ankityadav33 will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ankityadav33

item.title: Python 中的变量和数据类型 | Variables & Data Types in Python
item.description:
免责声明：如果您是程序员，那么您可以浏览整个部分。 稍后我们将深入探讨此部分。 我猜😉

在每种编程语言中，在某些时候，我们都需要存储一些数据。 这些数据存储在我们系统的内存中。 为了访问、使用和操作这些数据，我们将它们分配给一个变量。

简单来说，变量是您为用于存储实际数据的计算机内存位置指定的名称。 在python中，我们可以在任何地方声明变量。 不必所有变量都应位于代码文件的开头。 此外，要为变量赋值，我们可以执行以下操作：

 age = 5 name = "John Doe" height = 5.9 can_speak_french = False

为了编写更好的 python 代码，我们也可以遵循命名约定。 基本规则是：

  1. 每个变量都应该有适当的含义
  2. 尝试为变量使用简短而有意义的名称
  3. 如果变量包含多个有意义的单词，请使用下划线 (_) 分隔它们。

在编程中，我们有时会存储在程序的整个执行或生命周期中不会改变的值。 这种数据称为常量。

 ENVIRONMENT = "DEVELOPMENT"

常量遵循与任何普通变量相同的命名约定。 但是，有细微的变化。 所有常量仅以大写形式编写。

数据类型

数据类型是指将什么样的数据存储到变量或常量中。 数字：int、float、complex

  • 序列类型：str、list、tuple
  • 布尔值
  • 放
  • 字典

    • 数字

    我们可以将整数 (int)、浮点数和复数数据存储为数值数据类型。

    整数（整数）

    整数值在 python 中可以是任意长度。 整数的示例是 10、2、29、-20、-150 等。

     a = 5 print ( "The type of a" , type ( a )) # OUTPUT: The type of a <class 'int'>

    漂浮

    复杂的

    复数包含有序对，即 x + iy，其中 x 和 y 分别表示实部和虚部。

     c = 1 + 3j print ( "The type of c" , type ( c )) # OUTPUT: The type of c <class 'complex'>

    序列类型

    顾名思义，这种数据类型存储值/数据的序列。 此数据类型包含以下子类别：

    细绳

    字符串只是一个字符序列。 在 python 中，单个字符也被视为字符串。 要存储字符串数据，我们可以使用单引号、双引号或三引号。

     str1 = 'Use of double quotes' print ( str1 ) # OUTPUT: Use of double quotes str2 = """A multiline string""" print ( str2 ) # OUTPUT: A multiline # string str3 = "a" print ( type ( str3 )) # OUTPUT: <class 'str'>

    列表

    列表数据类型类似于数组，但不完全一样。 在数组中，我们只能存储一种类型的数据。 而在 python 列表中，我们可以将多种类型的数据存储到一个列表中。

     list1 = [ 0 , "Python" , "Program" , 1 ] print ( type ( list1 )) print ( list1 ) # OUTPUT: <class 'list'> # OUTPUT: [0, "Python", "Program", 1] list1 [ 0 ] = 2 print ( list1 ) # OUTPUT: [2, "Python", "Program", 1] list2 = list ([ 0 , "Python" , "Program" , 1 ]) print ( type ( list2 )) print ( list2 ) # OUTPUT: <class 'list'> # OUTPUT: [0, "Python", "Program", 1] list3 = [ 0 , "Python" , "Program" , 1 , 2 , 3 ] print ( list3 [ 2 ]) print ( list3 [ - 2 ]) # OUTPUT: Program # OUTPUT: 2

    元组

    元组数据类型类似于 python 中的列表。 列表和元组的主要区别在于，一旦我们创建了一个元组，我们就不能改变它的值。

     tuple1 = ( 0 , "Python" , "Program" , 1 ) print ( type ( tuple1 )) print ( tuple1 ) # OUTPUT: <class 'tuple'> # OUTPUT: (0, "Python", "Program", 1, 2, 3) tuple1 [ 1 ] = 5 # ERROR: # Traceback (most recent call last): # File "main.py", line 14, in <module> # tuple1[2] = "Python"; # TypeError: 'tuple' object does not support item assignment

    布尔值

    布尔值被认为是真实的。 它只有两个值。

    注意： True和False中的T和F必须大写。

     a1 = 5 b1 = 5 c1 = a1 == b1 print ( c1 ) # OUTPUT: True a2 = 5 b2 = "5" c2 = a2 == b2 print ( c2 ) # OUTPUT: False a3 = True print ( type ( a3 )) # OUTPUT: <class 'bool'>

    放

    可以像使用列表一样使用集合。 集合和列表之间的一个区别是，列表可以有重复的值，而集合不能。 下面的例子会更清楚。

    要创建 Set，我们必须使用set()函数或添加用花括号 {}包围的项目。 在日常编程中，我没有看到太多使用该集合的情况。 要创建空集，我们必须使用set()函数，空花括号{} 将不起作用。

     list1 = [ 1 , "Python" , "Program" , 2 , 1 , 2 ] print ( list1 ) # OUTPUT: [1, "Python", "Program", 2, 1, 2] set1 = set ([ 1 , "Python" , "Program" , 2 , 1 , 2 ]) print ( set1 ) # OUTPUT: {1, 2, 'Program', 'Python'} set2 = set ( "Hello There!" ) print ( set2 ) # OUTPUT: {'h', 'r', 'o', 'H', 'T', 'e', 'l', '!', ' '} set3 = { 1 , "Python" , "Program" , 2 , 1 , 2 } print ( set3 ) # OUTPUT: {1, 2, 'Program', 'Python'} set4 = {} print ( type ( set4 )) # OUTPUT: <class 'dict'> set5 = set () print ( type ( set5 )) # OUTPUT: <class 'set'>

    字典

    字典是以无序方式存储的键值对的集合。 如果您来自 Java 背景，您可以将其视为一种Map或Hashtable 。 字典中的项目用逗号 (,)分隔并括在花括号{}中。

    使用示例中显示的两种方法创建字典：

     dict1 = { 'Name' : 'Python' , 1 : [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ], 2 : "DICT" } print ( dict1 ) # OUTPUT: {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2: 'DICT'} dict2 = dict ({ 'Name' : 'Python' , 1 : [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ], 2 : "DICT" }) print ( dict2 ) # OUTPUT: {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2: 'DICT'} dict3 = dict ([( 'Name' , 'Python' ), ( 1 , [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ]), ( 2 , "DICT" )]) print ( dict3 ) # OUTPUT: {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2: 'DICT'} dict3 = { 'Name' : 'Python' , 'Last' : 'Programming' , 1 : [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ], 2 : "DICT" } print ( dict3 [ 'Name' ]) print ( dict3 [ 1 ]) print ( dict3 . get ( 2 )) # OUTPUT: Python # OUTPUT: [1, 2, 3, 4] # OUTPUT: "DICT"

    在字典中，我们通过使用键来获取或提取数据。 因此，当我们执行dict[1]时，从最后一段代码中，它会提取键为1的数据，即[1, 2, 3, 4] 。

    结论

    介绍本节只是为了展示 Python 中可用的不同数据类型。 有许多与这些数据类型相关的内置方法。 我们将在另一部分看到它们，我可以向您展示它们的一些实际用途。

    就是这样。 感谢您的阅读。

    我知道学习数据类型并不好玩，但我希望你对 Python 中的数据类型有所了解。 如果您有任何问题，请在Twitter或LinkedIn上与我联系。

    您可以订阅我的时事通讯以获得更多精彩的资源和文章。

    一旦暂停，sahilfruitwala 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，sahilfruitwala 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，sahilfruitwala 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 sahilfruitwala 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 sahilfruitwala 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 sahilfruitwala 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Disclaimer: If you are a programmer then you can just skim through this whole section. We will be exploring this section in-depth later. I guess 😉

    In every programming language, at some point, we need to store some data. This data is stored in our system's memory. To access, use and manipulate this data, we assign them to a variable.

    In simple terms, variables are the names you give to computer memory locations that are used for storing the actual data. In python, we can declare the variable anywhere. It is not necessary that all variables should be at the beginning of a code file. Also, to assign a value to the variable we can do the following:

    age = 5
name = "John Doe"
height = 5.9
can_speak_french = False

    To write better python code, we can follow naming convention as well. Basic rules are:

    1. Every variable should have a proper meaning
    2. Try to use short and meaningful names for variables
    3. If the variable contains more than one meaningful word use underscore ( _ ) to separate them.

    In programming, we sometimes store values that won't be changing throughout the execution or lifetime of the program. This kind of data is called constants.

    ENVIRONMENT = "DEVELOPMENT"

    Constants follow the same naming conventions as any normal variable. But, with minor changes. All constants are written in capital cases only.

    Data Types

    Data types refer to what kind of data will be stored into the variable or constant. Python can store and use many types of data. Python's built-in data types are categorized as follows:

    1. Numeric: int, float, complex
    2. Sequence Type: str, list, tuple
    3. Boolean
    4. Set
    5. Dictionary

    Numeric

    Python supports three kinds of numeric data. We can store integer (int), float and complex data as numeric data types.

    Integer(int)

    Integer values can be of any length in python. Example of integers are 10, 2, 29, -20, -150 etc.

    a = 5
print("The type of a", type(a)) # OUTPUT: The type of a <class 'int'>

    Float

    Float is used to store floating-point numbers like 1.9, 9.902, 15.2, etc. It is accurate up to 15 decimal points.

    b = 40.5
print("The type of b", type(b)) # OUTPUT: The type of b <class 'float'>

    Complex

    A complex number contains an ordered pair, i.e., x + iy where x and y denote the real and imaginary parts, respectively.

    c = 1+3j
print("The type of c", type(c)) # OUTPUT: The type of c <class 'complex'>

    Sequence Type

    As the name suggests, this data type stores sequence of values/data. This data type contains the following sub-categories:

    String

    The string is just a sequence of characters. A single character is also considered as a string in python. To store string data, we can use single, double, or triple quotes.

    str1 = 'Use of double quotes'
print(str1) # OUTPUT: Use of double quotes
 str2 = """A multiline
string"""
print(str2) # OUTPUT: A multiline
# string
 str3 = "a"
print(type(str3)) # OUTPUT: <class 'str'>

    List

    The list data type is similar to an array but not exactly. In an array, we can only store one type of data. Whereas, in python list, we can store multiple types of data into a single list.

    list1 = [0, "Python", "Program", 1]
print(type(list1))
print(list1)
# OUTPUT: <class 'list'>
# OUTPUT: [0, "Python", "Program", 1]

list1[0] = 2
print(list1)
# OUTPUT: [2, "Python", "Program", 1]

list2 = list([0, "Python", "Program", 1])
print(type(list2))
print(list2)
# OUTPUT: <class 'list'>
# OUTPUT: [0, "Python", "Program", 1]

list3 = [0, "Python", "Program", 1, 2, 3]
print(list3[2])
print(list3[-2])
# OUTPUT: Program
# OUTPUT: 2

    Tuple

    The tuple data type is similar to lists in python. The main difference between list and tuple is, once we created a tuple we can not change its value.

    tuple1 = (0, "Python", "Program", 1)
print(type(tuple1))
print(tuple1) # OUTPUT: <class 'tuple'>
# OUTPUT: (0, "Python", "Program", 1, 2, 3)
 tuple1[1] = 5
# ERROR:

# Traceback (most recent call last):
# File "main.py", line 14, in <module>
# tuple1[2] = "Python";
# TypeError: 'tuple' object does not support item assignment

    Boolean

    Boolean is considered as truthfulness. It has only two values.

    Note: T and F in True and False must be capital.

    a1 = 5
b1 = 5 c1 = a1==b1
print(c1)
# OUTPUT: True

a2 = 5
b2 = "5" c2 = a2==b2
print(c2)
# OUTPUT: False

a3 = True
print(type(a3))
# OUTPUT: <class 'bool'>

    Set

    Set can be used the same way we use lists. One difference between set and list is, the list can have duplicate values whereas the set can not. It will be more clear with the example shown below.

    To create a Set we have to use the set() function or add items surrounded by curly braces {}. In day-to-day programming, I have not seen much use of the set. Set has very small and specific use-cases.

    To create empty set we have to set() function, empty curly braces{} will not work.

    list1 = [1, "Python", "Program", 2, 1, 2]
print(list1)
# OUTPUT: [1, "Python", "Program", 2, 1, 2]

set1 = set([1, "Python", "Program", 2, 1, 2])
print(set1)
# OUTPUT: {1, 2, 'Program', 'Python'}

set2 = set("Hello There!")
print(set2)
# OUTPUT: {'h', 'r', 'o', 'H', 'T', 'e', 'l', '!', ' '}

set3 = {1, "Python", "Program", 2, 1, 2}
print(set3)
# OUTPUT: {1, 2, 'Program', 'Python'}

set4 = {}
print(type(set4))
# OUTPUT: <class 'dict'>

set5 = set()
print(type(set5))
# OUTPUT: <class 'set'>

    Dictionary

    Dictionary is a collection of key-value pairs stored in unorder fashion. If you are from a Java background you can see it as one kind of Map or Hashtable. The items in the dictionary are separated with the comma (,) and enclosed in the curly braces{}.

    To create a dictionary using two methods shown in the example:

    dict1 = {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2:"DICT"}
print(dict1)
# OUTPUT: {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2: 'DICT'}

dict2 = dict({'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2:"DICT"})
print(dict2)
# OUTPUT: {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2: 'DICT'}

dict3 = dict([('Name', 'Python'), (1, [1, 2, 3, 4]), (2,"DICT")])
print(dict3)
# OUTPUT: {'Name': 'Python', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2: 'DICT'}

dict3 = {'Name': 'Python', 'Last':'Programming', 1: [1, 2, 3, 4], 2:"DICT"}
print(dict3['Name'])
print(dict3[1])
print(dict3.get(2))
# OUTPUT: Python
# OUTPUT: [1, 2, 3, 4]
# OUTPUT: "DICT"

    In the dictionary, we get or extract data by using the key. So, from the last code when we do dict[1], it extracts data with the key 1 that is [1, 2, 3, 4].

    Conclusion

    This section is introduced to just showcase different data types available in python. There are many in-built methods associated with these data types. We will see them in another section where I can show you some practical use of them.

    That was it. Thank you for reading.

    I know it is not fun to learn about data types but I hope you got some knowledge of data types in Python. If you have any questions reach out to me on Twitter or LinkedIn.

    You can subscribe to my Newsletter for more amazing resources and articles.
